title: Falls Prevention for Area Agencies on Aging

seo.title: Falls Prevention for Area Agencies on Aging | Able Care
seo.description: Objective falls screening for AAAs and senior centers. Prove the impact of evidence-based programs like Matter of Balance and Otago, identify beneficiary needs, and defend grant funding.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Older adult and family member at home, supported by an AAA community program
blocks.headline: Objective falls screening for community-based aging services.
blocks.subtitle: Prove the impact of Matter of Balance, Otago and other evidence-based programs. Identify beneficiary needs. Defend the grant funding that pays for it all.

blocks.heading: What Able Assess Offers Area Agencies on Aging

metrics.name: Program outcomes evidence
metrics.whatItMeasures: Screen participants at the start and end of evidence-based programs to provide objective documentation of impact — defensible when reporting back to funders.

metrics.name: Beneficiary needs identification
metrics.whatItMeasures: Empower non-clinical staff to identify falls risk and refer participants to physical therapy or other support services they're entitled to under their insurance.

metrics.name: Grant funding defense
metrics.whatItMeasures: Strengthen applications for new funding and protect current funding by showing that the programs you run actually benefit participants.

blocks.heading: How it works at an AAA

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: A program coordinator or non-clinical staff member runs a five-minute baseline assessment at intake or at the start of an evidence-based program.

steps.title: Score
steps.description: Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go are scored against normative data instantly.

steps.title: Act
steps.description: Flag participants who may need PT or other support services and connect them to the benefits already covered by their health insurance.

steps.title: Report
steps.description: Re-screen at the end of programs. Objective before-and-after data goes straight into the impact reports your funders see.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.whatItMeasures: Full body strength proxy. Predicts all-cause mortality and frailty in older adults.

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.whatItMeasures: Lower-limb power and balance — a measure many evidence-based programs already track.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.whatItMeasures: Walking speed over a standardized 4-meter distance. The 'sixth vital sign' of aging.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.whatItMeasures: Falls risk in under a minute. Validated for community-based screening.

blocks.heading: The numbers that matter to community programs

stats.label: Reduction in falls with objective screening
Clinical certifications required to run it

testimonials.quote: We can finally show our funders that the programs they pay for are working — with numbers, not anecdotes.
Identifying participants who needed PT was the easiest referral pathway we've ever had.

blocks.heading: Common questions from AAAs and community centers

faqs.question: Does Able Assess work with evidence-based programs like Matter of Balance or Otago?
faqs.answer: Yes. Run a five-minute baseline at the start of the program and again at the end. The before-and-after data gives you defensible proof of impact for funder reports and grant applications.

faqs.question: Can non-clinical staff run the assessment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Able Assess was built for non-clinical staff — program coordinators, wellness coordinators and volunteers can all run the protocol. No clinical certification required.

faqs.question: We're worried about budget. How does this protect grant funding?
faqs.answer: Able Assess helps you prove the benefit of your programs objectively — which protects current grant funding and strengthens applications for new funding. The data is exactly what funders are increasingly asking for.

faqs.question: How does it help us identify beneficiaries who need more support?
faqs.answer: A standardized screen flags participants at elevated falls risk. Staff can then refer them to physical therapy or other support services — fulfilling the AAA mandate to help beneficiaries access their health insurance benefits.

faqs.question: What hardware do we need?
faqs.answer: The sensor, pouch and measuring tape are provided as a kit. Everything pairs via Bluetooth with any iOS or Android smartphone or tablet. No additional infrastructure required.

faqs.question: Where is participant data stored?
faqs.answer: ISO 27001 certified, HIPAA aligned and GDPR compliant. Data residency options available for US deployments.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your community programs.
blocks.bodyText: Book a walkthrough with a clinician who has deployed it for AAAs and community centers.
